title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men were charged with kidnapping and assault for allegedly attacking a man near Winter Park.
The suspects, aged 21-34, reportedly restrained the victim with an extension cord after beating him.
All four, identified by Nantucket police, were released on bail and face arraignment on Monday.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Maria Rosales and David Ardila were arrested by ICE at Logan Airport on July 10.
Both individuals are awaiting immigration decisions: Rosales for DACA renewal and Ardila for an asylum application.
Their lawyer, Todd Pomerleau, reported approximately 10 similar immigration arrests at Logan Airport recently.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Kevin Slane suggests five movies to watch after Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey'.
'Interstellar' and 'Lawrence of Arabia' are recommended for thematic similarities to 'The Odyssey'.
The list includes 'O Brother, Where Art Thou?', 'The Searchers', and 'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie'.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox extended their win streak to 11 games, sweeping the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader.
Jake Bennett pitched six innings of one-hit ball to secure a 10-0 victory in Game 1 of the doubleheader.
In Game 2, Wilyer Abreu's two home runs led the Red Sox past the Rays with a 5-3 win.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitchers Ranger Suarez and Connelly Early show progress in their recoveries and could return soon.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo receive positive rehabilitation updates, with potential season returns.
Several players, including Roman Anthony and Garrett Crochet, have no recovery updates or return timetables.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit three times, including a home run, contributing to the Red Sox's Game 1 victory against the Rays.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 in the doubleheader, consistently getting on base and pressuring the Rays' pitchers.
Jake Bennett delivered 6 scoreless innings in Game 1, aiding bullpen conservation for the Red Sox's second game.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots owner Robert Kraft expressed support for head coach Mike Vrabel amid offseason controversies during an ESPN appearance.
Vrabel faced allegations of an affair with NFL reporter Dianna Russini, leading to her resignation from The Athletic.
The Patriots, under Vrabel, reached the Super Bowl last season and will begin training camp next weekend in Foxborough.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "The Hunt for the Wilderpeople," featuring late actor Sam Neill, is now streaming on Prime Video and Plex.
Horror film "Obsession" has grossed over $400 million and is available on Peacock.
Apple TV's new series "Lucky," starring Anya Taylor-Joy, centers on a Las Vegas crime family.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin violated NH Civil Rights Act in a 2024 bias-motivated assault on a transgender woman in Concord.
Lufkin was fined $5,000 with restrictions on contacting the victim and barred from her home and workplace.
He received suspended sentences for second-degree and simple assault after pleading guilty to the charges.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox' trade plans are 'fluid' as the August 3 deadline approaches, according to ESPN's Jeff Passan.
Despite a recent 10-game winning streak, Boston is still undecided on whether to acquire or sell players.
Trade discussions include the potential move of All-Star closer Aroldis Chapman, but a deal is increasingly unlikely.
